Off Calendar Jan-16-2013 Off Calendar

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-28-2012 Continued To Jan-29-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Petition To Approve Compromise Of Disputed Claim Of Person With Disability
Off Calendar Nov-27-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for a Hearing on a Compromise of Disputed Claim of a Person with a Disability.
Department 514, Judge: Donald Sullivan, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, CSR: Sonia Wong, #11288.
Valerie N. Rose, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff(s) Petitioner: Vivian Li-Huey Liou stepmother of Emily Liou.
After testimony and consideration of the evidence presented, the Petition is granted and compromise is approved as follows:
Total Settlement: $75,000.00
Attorney's Fees: $23,632.82
Reimbursement for Medidal and all other expenses paid by the petitioner or the petitioner's attorney in the amount of $4,101.53
for a total allowance of $27,734.35
A net balance of $47,265.65 to be deposited into the existing trust account entitled "emily Liou First Party Special Needs Trust" created pursuant to an Order of January 15, 2010 by the Honorable George A. Miranm of the San Mateo Superior Court and overseen by the Probate court, Case Number 119504. See attachment 7c(2)(c) of Order.
Status Hearing Scheduled on February 5, 2013 at 9:00 AM, in Department 514 for filing of receipt of Deposit of money into Special Needs Trust. No appearance is required if the receipt is filed and courtesy copy lodge with Depart. 514 prior to Hearing.

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-13-2012 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Dec-04-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Pltf And For Order Imposing Monetary Sanctions Against Pltf; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Jun-27-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ANTHEM BLUE CROSS LIFE & HEALTH INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITIONS OF PLAINTIFFS AND FOR ORDER IMPOSING MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF.
Appearances: Horace W. Green of Green & Humbert (837.5433) for defendant and moving party; Douglas S. Saeltzer of Walkup Melodia (981.7210) for plaintiff and responding party.
The court made a personal disclosure, and no objections were raised. Discussed and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: motion to compel is granted in part. The court requires that defendant do one of the following: either depose plaintiffs at a time when they are in the country. The location of the deposition may be chosen by defendant within the legal parameters. If defendant wishes to pick a time when plaintiffs are not in the country for deposition, then defendant must pay for plaintiffs to travel here or travel to China to take the plaintiff's deposition. Requests for sanctions by both parties are denied. Further, the parties agreed that Mr. Saeltzer will provide Mr. Green with written confirmation of the dates of plaintiffs' availability for deposition. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Not reported
